title: Using MyAIforOne
description: A foundational orientation to the platform — how it works, what you can do, and the philosophy behind it. Start here.

modules.title: Your First Conversation
modules.agentInstructions: This is about the hub — the central interface. Make sure the learner understands that hub is not just a chatbot, it's a command center with full platform access. Keep it practical, not a lecture.

steps.title: Meet the Hub
steps.content:
The **Hub** is your primary interface to the platform. It's not just a chatbot — it has access to the entire platform through a set of tools called MCPs (more on those later).

From a single conversation with Hub, you can:
- Create and configure agents
- Assign tasks, set goals, run automations
- Search your files and memory
- Get a full view of everything happening across all your agents

You don't need to navigate menus for most things. Just talk to Hub.

The Home screen (`/`) is where you start. If Hub is selected (or no agent is mentioned), your message goes to Hub by default.
verificationQuestions: What makes Hub different from a regular chatbot?
Name two things you can do from a Hub conversation without touching any menus.

steps.title: Send Your First Real Message
steps.content:
Time to try it. Go to the Home screen and send Hub a message with a real task — not just 'hello'. Some ideas:

- *"What agents do I have set up?"*
- *"Show me what's on my task list"*
- *"I want to create a new agent for [something you do regularly]"*
- *"What can you help me with?"*

Have at least 3 back-and-forth messages. Notice how Hub responds — it knows about your platform, your agents, your config.

steps.title: Talking to Specific Agents
steps.content:
You can direct a message to any agent using `@alias` — for example, `@gym` or `@coach`.

If you type `@` in the chat input, an autocomplete list of all your agents will appear. Select one and your message goes directly to that agent instead of Hub.

This is how the platform routes: mention → specific agent. No mention → Hub (default).

You can also click any agent tile on the Home screen to auto-insert their `@alias` into the input.

modules.title: Growing Your AI Skills
modules.agentInstructions: This module is about the Gym — make it come alive. Focus on the 6 preset actions as the entry points. The learner is literally using the Gym right now, so make it meta and engaging.

steps.title: What is the AI Gym?
steps.content:
The **AI Gym** is your personal training ground for getting better at using AI.

It tracks your skill level across 5 dimensions:
- **Application** — Are you using AI for real work?
- **Communication** — Are your prompts actually good?
- **Knowledge** — Do you understand how agents work?
- **Orchestration** — Can you coordinate multiple agents?
- **Craft** — Can you build and tune your own agents?

The Gym observes your activity over time, scores you on each dimension, and gives you targeted coaching. The coach agent — your personal trainer — is what you're talking to right now.

Switch to the Gym at any time using the **Work / AI Gym** toggle at the top.
verificationQuestions: Name the 5 skill dimensions the Gym tracks.
How does the Gym know how skilled you are — does it quiz you, or observe you?

steps.title: The 6 Preset Actions
steps.content:
Above the chat input in Gym mode, you'll see a bar labeled **PRESET ACTIONS** with 6 buttons. These are your quick entry points into coaching:

| Button | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Where do I stand?** | Fresh snapshot of your 5 dimension scores right now |
| **How was this week?** | What you actually did — sessions, agents, progress |
| **What are my gaps?** | Specific weaknesses: low scores, unused features, struggles |
| **What should I focus on?** | One clear recommendation for what to work on next |
| **Create a learning plan** | A structured 2-week plan based on your gaps |
| **Create a guide** | Start a conversation to build your own guide |

Think of these as the most common things you'd want from a coach — packaged as one click.

steps.title: Guides & Programs
steps.content:
In the left sidebar of the Gym, you'll find three panels:

- **Guides** — Structured learning programs you can work through step by step. Platform guides ship with the product. Coach guides are created for you personally during sessions.
- **My Growth** — Your dimension scores, progress history, milestones, and streak.
- **Create** — A form to kick off guide creation yourself.

Guides are the main curriculum. Each one has modules and steps. When you're in a guide, you can ask the coach questions about any step — it's a live conversation alongside the content.

modules.title: Projects, Goals & Tasks
modules.agentInstructions: Three primitives that together cover how work gets tracked and automated. Keep explanations concrete — what each one is for, not just what it is.

steps.title: Tasks — Finite Work
steps.content:
A **Task** is a finite piece of work assigned to an agent.

Every agent has a task list. You (or another agent) can create tasks for any agent. Tasks have a title, description, priority, and status (open → in progress → done).

Use tasks for anything with a clear endpoint: *"Draft Q2 email"*, *"Research competitors"*, *"Set up Slack integration"*.

You can manage tasks from the Agent Dashboard (`/org` → click an agent) or by asking Hub: *"Create a task for @[agent] to do X"*.
verificationQuestions: What's the difference between a task and a regular chat message?
How would you ask Hub to create a task for another agent?

steps.title: Goals — Autonomous, Always-On
steps.content:
A **Goal** is an autonomous objective that an agent works on independently on a recurring schedule — without you having to trigger it each time.

Goals run on a heartbeat (a cron schedule). Each time the heartbeat fires, the agent wakes up, reads its goal instructions, and works toward them.

Example: *"Every morning, check for new emails about Project X and summarize them"*.

Goals are what turn agents from tools you prompt into assistants that work for you in the background. You can toggle them on/off at any time.
verificationQuestions: What makes a Goal different from a Task?
What is a 'heartbeat' in the context of a Goal?

steps.title: Projects — Multi-Agent Initiatives
steps.content:
A **Project** is a container for long-running, multi-faceted work that spans multiple agents.

Unlike tasks (one agent, one deliverable), a project bundles together:
- An **owner agent** responsible for driving it forward
- **Team members** — other agents contributing
- A **plan** (markdown)
- Linked tasks, artifacts, and context

Projects live at `/projects`. Use them when you have something too big for a single task — a product launch, a research initiative, a client engagement.

Hub can create and manage projects: *"Create a project for [initiative], assign @[agent] as owner"*.

modules.title: The Lab
modules.agentInstructions: The Lab is where users build and run custom apps and tools. Keep this light — it's an orientation, not a deep dive.

steps.title: What's in the Lab?
steps.content:
The **Lab** (`/lab`) is where you build and run custom apps and tools on top of your agents.

Instead of chatting with an agent, you can create purpose-built interfaces — forms, dashboards, utilities — that call agents under the hood.

Examples:
- A **contract review form** that sends a doc to your legal agent and returns a structured summary
- A **daily briefing app** that pulls from multiple agents and presents a clean digest
- A **prompt tester** that lets you iterate on agent instructions quickly

Apps in the Lab are yours — they live locally, they use your agents, your data stays on your machine.

Ask Hub: *"Create a Lab app for [use case]"* to get started.

modules.title: What Else Is Here
modules.agentInstructions: Quick tour of the remaining platform surfaces. One sentence per thing — the goal is orientation, not depth. Direct them to ask Hub for more on anything.

steps.title: A Quick Tour
steps.content:
Here's what else is on the platform — quick orientation only. Ask Hub to go deeper on any of these.

**Library** (`/library`) — A collection of guides, skills, and resources your agents can reference. Think of it as your knowledge base.

**Marketplace** (`/marketplace`) — Browse and install pre-built agents, skills, MCP integrations, and prompt templates. Everything installs in one click.

**Monitor** (`/monitor`) — A health dashboard showing all agent activity, costs, errors, and uptime. Your control tower.

**Admin** (`/admin`) — Configure channels (Telegram, Slack, Discord, WhatsApp, iMessage), manage accounts, and adjust platform settings.

**Org** (`/org`) — Your full agent roster. Create, configure, and manage every agent from one place.

The nav bar at the top is your map. You can get to anything from Hub too — just ask.

modules.title: The Platform & Its Philosophy
modules.agentInstructions: This is the 'why' behind the platform. It's not a feature tour — it's about values. Make it feel meaningful. This is what makes MyAIforOne different from every other AI product.

steps.title: Your Data Stays on Your Machine
steps.content:
Everything on this platform lives on your computer — in your folders, under your control.

Your agents' memory, conversation history, files, tasks, and configuration: all stored locally on your machine. Not in someone else's cloud. Not on a server you don't control.

This means:
- You can back it up, move it, or delete it — your call
- No company is storing your conversations to train models
- Your data is yours, permanently

This is a deliberate design choice. Most AI products make your data their data. MyAIforOne inverts that.
verificationQuestions: Where does your conversation history and agent memory actually live?
What does 'your data stays on your machine' mean practically — give one example.

steps.title: Privacy by Default, Open Source by Design
steps.content:
Today the platform runs on **Claude** (Anthropic's model) by default. That means your messages do get sent to Anthropic's API to generate responses — just like using Claude.ai directly.

But the platform is built to be model-agnostic. As open-source models mature and become capable enough for everyday tasks, you'll be able to flip to running a model **entirely on your own hardware** — no API calls, no data leaving your machine, ever.

The multi-model architecture is already built in. The path to 100% local, 100% private AI is already there. It's a configuration change, not a rebuild.

When you're ready to go fully offline, the platform is ready too.

steps.title: Built for You, Not for Everyone
steps.content:
Most software is built for the average user. MyAIforOne is built for **you specifically**.

The agents you create know your work, your context, your preferences. They remember what you told them last week. They're configured for your projects, your tools, your way of working.

This is what **hyperpersonalization** means in practice:
- An agent for your job that knows your company's terminology
- An agent for your creative projects that knows your voice
- An agent for your health goals that knows your history
- An agent for your finances that knows your accounts

None of these exist until you build them. And when you build them, they're yours — no one else has access to them, no one can see them, and they get smarter the more you use them.

The future of AI isn't one giant model that serves everyone the same way. It's deeply personal agents that know you — and only you.
verificationQuestions: What does 'hyperpersonalization' mean in the context of this platform?
Give one example of a personal agent you could create that would be useful specifically for your life or work.
